reasoning: The Oklahoma City Thunder (1st seed) are playing the Indiana Pacers (6th seed) in the 2025 NBA Finals. Before the series began, the Thunder were massive favorites at sportsbooks (around -650 or 86.6% implied probability), although Polymarket had them at 0.79 on May 29. After a surprise one-point loss at home in Game 1, the Thunder bounced back in Game 2 with a convincing 123-107 victory to tie the series 1-1. While they have lost home-court advantage for the remainder of the series (with 3 away games and 2 home games left), they remain significantly stronger on paper. Calculating their win probability based on single-game win likelihoods (-11 favorites at home in G2 suggests ~80% win probability at home and ~60% away), their mathematical chance of winning 3 of the next 5 games is around 81-82%. Accounting for potential variance and the pre-series Polymarket price of 79%, 80% is a well-calibrated robust estimate.
